This service manages the following components:
This service is based on Cegeka Cloud Housing services.
Cegeka advises in the setup of the virtual machines in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Infrastructure and in line with the best practices.
This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants).
The service makes use of the below storage performance flavors (S-tiers):
Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Infrastructure on the agreed platform ensuring its correct working, in line with VMware best practices.
Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Infrastructure and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows.

pricing:
Changes are billed on a monthly basis.
Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact.
Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.

Service Compute services IaaS Unique ID PRD.0.L.108 Service Model Infrastructure management This service is dependant of other layers which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the full stack management.

The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage the shared infrastructure to host virtual machines (The Infrastructure). Virtual machines can be used for Windows OS systems, Linux OS systems or virtual appliances.
The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.
The virtual machines are provided from a managed VMware virtualisation platform.

This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features.
The following optional features are possible with the Service:
Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the virtualization environment in order to perform the Service.

Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Infrastructure. Automation is applied where possible.
Proactive management
Housekeeping includes following regular checks:
Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA.
Patching
The service includes patching of the underlaying virtualization environment and used hardware. Patching is transparent for the customer due to the nature of the virtualization environment.
Life Cycle Management
Cegeka monitors the Infrastructure and updates the underlaying hardware and software transparent for the customer. Cegeka follows the constructor’s normal end of support where applicable.

Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Infrastructure.
Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service.
Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management.

Management of technical users
The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies.
Detection of security incidents
The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies.
Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders.
SIEM
SIEM is part of the Cegeka internal service and not deliverable to the Customer.
Underlaying Platform hardening
Not applicable for this Service.
Backup and restore of the servers on the Cegeka Cloud Compute services is part of OS management and described in the relevant service description.

Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals).
Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals).
n/a

Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). | request type | Description | Lead time | | Service Requests | (no specific Service Requests for this Service) | | | | Request a new virtual server for a virtual appliance | 5 working days | | | Add system resources (Mem, CPU) | 2 working hours | | | Decommission a virtual server | 2 months (1 month power off) (next month actual removal) | | | Install new VM | 3 working days | | | Reboot Windows VM | 0,5 working hours | | | Extend disk – Windows | 8 hours | | | Add disk – Windows | 8 hours | | Normal Changes | Any other request. (e.g. determine/change host affinity (VIRP setting as documented in the CMDB) | Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request. |
request type Description Lead time Service Requests (no specific Service Requests for this Service) Request a new virtual server for a virtual appliance 5 working days Add system resources (Mem, CPU) 2 working hours Decommission a virtual server 2 months (1 month power off) (next month actual removal) Install new VM 3 working days Reboot Windows VM 0,5 working hours Extend disk – Windows 8 hours Add disk – Windows 8 hours Normal Changes Any other request. (e.g. determine/change host affinity (VIRP setting as documented in the CMDB) Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.
Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service.

The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:
Cloud Housing services (where the Cloud Compute Services reside)

These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Monthly reporting will be provided to inform on the availability and meeting the SLA’s and to provide insights on the capacity and expected extensions required.
Specific reports are provided:

Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service.
Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service.
In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

This service is delivered with:
Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues):
VMware (Broadcom)
Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party):
(none)
n/a

The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: | Cross-Functional Service | Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services | | n/a | |
Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services n/a The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.
The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. The actual mapping of the operational service instances towards these KPI’s are documented on the Service Delivery site under ‘Operational documentation’.
